title: UK Fuel Prices Go Down After Going Up
summary: In the UK, the prices for petrol and diesel have started to fall a little bit after going up for many weeks. This is the first time prices have dropped since a war involving the US, Israel, and Iran. The RAC, a group that helps drivers, says that prices went down on Thursday and Friday. Diesel went down by 0.6p and petrol by 0.3p per litre. Now, diesel costs just under 191p a litre, and petrol is just under 158p a litre. Even with this small drop, filling a car with diesel costs about £26 more than in late February, and petrol costs nearly £14 more. The RAC thinks prices might fall more in the coming days because wholesale costs are lower. Drivers are happy to see prices going down after they went up a lot. The price rises over six weeks made diesel go from 142p to nearly 192p a litre and petrol from 133p to over 158p a litre. Fuel prices are still lower than in summer 2022 after Russia invaded Ukraine. Many people are worried about fuel costs because it makes living more expensive, with 75% of people saying fuel prices are a reason for higher costs in March, up from 38% in February. Experts say this shows people are anxious about global events affecting their money.

another type of fuel used in some cars and trucks

the price that shops pay for fuel before selling it to people

Why does the RAC think prices might fall more?
What percentage of people said fuel prices increased living costs in March?

background_read: This article talks about fuel prices in the UK. Fuel is what makes cars go, and it costs money to buy it. Sometimes, prices go up or down because of things happening in the world, like wars or changes in how fuel is made and sold. The RAC is an organization that watches fuel prices and helps drivers. When fuel prices go up, it can be hard for people to afford things, and when they go down, it can help save money. This helps us understand why prices change and how it affects everyone.
Article_Structure: WHO: The RAC and drivers in the UK
WHAT: Petrol and diesel prices have fallen slightly after weeks of rises
WHY: Because wholesale costs have dropped and people are relieved after price increases

perspectives.description: Drivers are happy to see fuel prices going down after they went up a lot, making it easier to afford driving.
